title: veronica agrestis var. pulchella
intro: Veronica agrestis var. pulchella, also known as the wood speedwell, is a perennial plant species in the plantain family (Plantaginaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating skin conditions, and aiding in wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be used to treat various ailments.
